Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. AyaFlow: A high-performance, eBPF-based network traffic analyzer written in Rust 48 upvotes, 3 comments
  2. Founder of GitLab battles cancer by founding companies 1305 upvotes, 249 comments
  3. Pretext: TypeScript library for multiline text measurement and layout 89 upvotes, 12 comments
  4. Show HN: QuickBEAM – run JavaScript as supervised Erlang/OTP processes 33 upvotes, 4 comments
  5. Show HN: Create a full language server in Go with 3.17 spec support 70 upvotes, 14 comments
  6. The Failure of the Thermodynamics of Computation (2010) 41 upvotes, 7 comments
  7. The loneliness of A Room of One’s Own 33 upvotes, 6 comments
  8. The RISE RISC-V Runners: free, native RISC-V CI on GitHub 70 upvotes, 18 comments
  9. The rise and fall of IBM's 4 Pi aerospace computers: an illustrated history 34 upvotes, 7 comments
  10. I turned my Kindle into my own personal newspaper 148 upvotes, 51 comments
  11. Police used AI facial recognition to wrongly arrest TN woman for crimes in ND 219 upvotes, 82 comments
  12. Full network of clitoral nerves mapped out for first time 102 upvotes, 34 comments
  13. Nitrile and latex gloves may cause overestimation of microplastics 442 upvotes, 188 comments
  14. Netscape News Feed Straight Out of the Late 00s 30 upvotes, 7 comments
  15. Voyager 1 runs on 69 KB of memory and an 8-track tape recorder 196 upvotes, 87 comments
  16. A nearly perfect USB cable tester 230 upvotes, 108 comments
  17. C++26 is done ISO C++ standards meeting, Trip Report 61 upvotes, 24 comments
  18. Neovim 0.12.0 106 upvotes, 50 comments
  19. Observations from carbon dioxide monitoring 9 upvotes, 1 comments
  20. LinkedIn uses 2.4 GB RAM across two tabs 418 upvotes, 266 comments
  21. Alzheimer's disease mortality among taxi and ambulance drivers (2024) 199 upvotes, 131 comments
  22. The bot situation on the internet is worse than you could imagine 153 upvotes, 103 comments
  23. Miasma: A tool to trap AI web scrapers in an endless poison pit 233 upvotes, 180 comments
  24. Show HN: BreezePDF – Free, in-browser PDF editor 35 upvotes, 22 comments
  25. Cuts in publishing and book reviewing imperil the future of narrative nonfiction 44 upvotes, 31 comments
  26. First Western Digital, now Sony: The tech giant suspends SD card sales 38 upvotes, 29 comments
  27. The Epistemology of Microphysics 16 upvotes, 9 comments
  28. Show HN: Sheet Ninja – Google Sheets as a CRUD Back End for Vibe Coders 57 upvotes, 63 comments
  29. When do we become adults, really? 49 upvotes, 68 comments
  30. Typing and Keyboards 12 upvotes, 12 comments

Last generated 3:30am, 30 March 2026. Source here.
